chi: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Chinese.

chiton: noun. A loose woolen tunic worn by men and women in Ancient Greece and in Ancient Rome.
chug: noun. A dull, fairly quick explosive or percussive sound, as if made by a labouring engine.
chuting: verb. present participle and gerund of chute
cog: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chong.
coign: noun. A projecting corner or angle; a cornerstone.
coin: noun. A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
coting: verb. present participle and gerund of cote

cunt: noun. The female genitalia, especially the vulva.
cut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Teutila Cuicatec.
cutin: noun. A waxy polymer of hydroxy acids that is the main constituent of plant cuticle.
git: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gitxsan.
gnu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gnau.
got: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gothic.
gothic: adjective. Alternative letter-case form of Gothic.
gout: noun. An extremely painful inflammation of joints, especially of the big toe, caused by a metabolic defect resulting in the accumulation of
gun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mbya Guarani.
gut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Maléku Jaíka.
hin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Hindi.
hit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Hittite.
hog: noun. Any animal belonging to the Suidae family of mammals, especially the pig, the warthog, and the boar.
hognut: noun. A pignut or hickory tree (Carya glabra of family Juglandaceae).
hon: noun. A term of endearment; Honey, sweetheart
hot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hote.
hut: noun. A small, simple one- storey dwelling or shelter, often with just one room, and generally built of readily available local materials.
ich: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Etkywan.
icon: noun. An image, symbol, picture, portrait, or other representation, usually as an object of religious devotion.
inch: noun. An English unit of length equal to 1/12 of a foot or 2.54 cm, conceived as roughly the width of a thumb.
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
itch: noun. A sensation felt on an area of the skin that causes a person or animal to want to scratch said area.
night: noun. The time when the Sun is below the horizon when the sky is dark.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.

thin: adjective. Having little thickness or extent from one surface to its opposite.
thing: noun. That which is considered to exist as a separate entity, object, quality or concept.
thong: noun. A narrow strip of material, typically leather, used to fasten, bind, or secure objects.
thouing: verb. present participle and gerund of thou

tic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tira.

tonic: adjective. Pertaining to tension, especially of the muscles.

ugh: interjection. Used to express repugnance, disgust, or annoyance.
unit: noun. Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
